mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, lifting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in real consumer spending indicates supply chain bottlenecks meeting resilient demand, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, a sequential decline in housing starts signals tightening financial conditions that propagate to corporate capex and equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in > 55% of out-of-sample windows during high-rate periods, or the forward return differential between declining and rising HOUST months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real consumer spending reflects underlying economic resilience that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exhibit a monotonic relationship with RSAFS momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below its 12-month moving average, it indicates a supply-side contraction that historically precedes equity drawdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and a cut in the federal funds rate signals a policy response to weakening labor markets, which historically marks the bottom of equity cycles and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized, a concurrent drop in housing starts confirms a demand-driven slowdown that compresses equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in > 55% of out-of-sample windows during deceleration phases, or the forward return differential between declining and rising HOUST months is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
